title: Podman user mode breaks because homed changes uid on different host
body:
### Issue Description

Homed's advantage of being portable is restricted because podman attaches itself to the host uid. Removing or resetting podman settings as stated below

- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11377
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15310
- https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18633

every time I switch host is not an ideal solution. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Change uid
2. Log out, log in
3. Enter container
 


### Describe the results you received

No previous containers on home directory can be used. Containers must be erased because the data base's uid doesn't match current uid, resulting in having to be re-downloaded and re configured to use every time switching host OS. 

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the the user containers to be usable by the user and not attached to the host OS.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Podman cannot work under homed since each file in the storage has the wrong ownership.  One possibility would be to chown each file in the storage to follow the new mappings, but that is a lot of complexity for something that could be handled outside of Podman as well.
